rationale: Both candidates converge on the same core design critique (derive default_components from existing declared metadata like _dit_modules rather than hardcoding), which is grounded but only tangentially related to the actual ground-truth concern (dsocek's suggestion to derive naming from _packed_modules_mapping/stacked_params_mapping for fused-projection renames), so recall is low and similar for both. X adds a second well-grounded, actionable finding (text-encoder LoRA silent no-op) with a concrete warning-log suggestion, and presents everything as a clean, human-readable review with clear verdict framing. Y's output is a raw harness/tool-log dump (fetch state, gate report, assumptions list, duplicated diff text) that buries its one actionable comment in noise, hurting actionability despite comparable technical grounding.
